content:
CHURN REPORT
Customer: Irongate Construction
Customer ID: C011
Contract ID: K011
Account Executive: Tom Rivera
Contract End Date: March 31, 2024
Report Date: April 8, 2024

REASON FOR CHURN
Irongate Construction did not renew their DataSync Pro Starter contract after one year. The account was flagged as at-risk as early as November 2023 following consistently low login activity and a missed QBR in Q4 2023.

CONTRIBUTING FACTORS
Irongate's primary use case was document sharing across job sites. They had hoped DataSync Pro would replace their existing paper-based handoff process, but the mobile experience did not meet the needs of their field crews who were working in low-connectivity environments. This limitation was raised on a call in October 2023 by their office manager, Renee Kowalski.

Tom Rivera escalated the mobile connectivity concern to the product team in November 2023 but no roadmap commitment was available in time for the renewal conversation. Irongate's decision-maker, VP of Operations Greg Halloran, indicated he had been shopping alternatives since December.

No discount or concession was offered during the renewal conversation as the account size ($10,800 ARR) did not meet the threshold for retention investment per internal policy.

COMPETITIVE LOSS
Lost to Fieldlink, a construction-specific mobile platform with offline capabilities.

LESSONS LEARNED
Vertical-specific feature gaps are a meaningful churn risk in industries with field-based workforces. Consider flagging accounts in construction, utilities, and logistics for early product fit reviews.

FINAL ARR LOST: $10,800
